  "rationale": "Four containment gaps detected: (1) Section 4.11 SovereignAuditResponseV1 specifies epoch-order proof requirement when request.required_epoch_id is set, but Section 6.5 verification algorithm does not enforce it, creating audit-temporal bypass risk. (2) Section 4.15 HardwareKillActivationReceiptV1 allows null stop_order_id for physical activations but lacks reconciliation requirements before resume, creating unbound-activation drift risk. (3) Section 4.11b AuditBypassManifestV1 is referenced in constraints but not verified in Section 6.5 algorithm, allowing mediated-only audit responses to pass. (4) Section 0.3 defines constitutional admissibility precheck booleans but acceptance criteria (Section 10.5) do not verify their enforcement, allowing governance evidence to omit humanitarian/containment precheck reporting.  5. If confidential payloads are returned, `declassification_receipt_hashes` MUST be non-empty.

-6. Reuse of non-null `(stop_order_id, challenge_nonce_echo)` across distinct `activation_id` values is a replay defect and MUST fail closed.
-7. Any null-bound activation MUST produce a reconciliation receipt before any sovereign resume is admissible.
+6. Replay guards:
+   a. Reuse of non-null `(stop_order_id, challenge_nonce_echo)` across distinct `activation_id` values is a replay defect and MUST fail closed.
+   b. Reuse of non-null `(actuator_id, activation_edge_tick, challenge_nonce_echo)` across distinct `activation_id` values is a replay defect and MUST fail closed.
+7. Any null-bound activation (where `stop_order_id` is null) MUST produce a reconciliation receipt proving activation binding to subsequent epoch state before any `SovereignResumeOrderV1` admission is permitted.
